Main objective: Remove tank/global-solver assumptions from the plan and lock the wrapper-level scope.
Exit criteria: Scope statement explicitly says no dynamic internal tanks, no shared header inventory, no four-bed RHS, no core physics edits.
Go / no-go risks: No-go if implementation still requires dynamic tanks or a global four-bed solve.

Main objective: Convert Yang cycle description into an executable manifest.
Exit criteria: Manifest reproduces A/B/C/D step sequences and handles source duration spans explicitly.
Key artifacts: Yang manifest sheet; normalized manifest spec.
Go / no-go risks: No-go if direct-transfer counterparts cannot be identified unambiguously.

Main objective: Define direct coupling rules independent of bed row order.
Exit criteria: All direct-transfer operations have donor/receiver/end metadata and static validators.
Go / no-go risks: No-go if a pair can be inferred only from table order or native defaults.

Main objective: Prove persistent named states can enter and exit temporary toPSAil cases correctly.
Exit criteria: Round-trip and writeback tests pass for adjacent and crossed pairs; non-participants remain unchanged.
Key artifacts: State container; case-builder contract; smoke logs.
Go / no-go risks: No-go if global bed identity is lost inside local bed 1/2 calls.

Main objective: Run small EQI/EQII/PP/AD&PP-BF cases without dynamic tanks.
Exit criteria: Pair-local conservation, endpoint, direction, and ledger tests pass.
Key artifacts: Toy run logs; conservation residual report.
Go / no-go risks: No-go if conservation only closes by adding a hidden tank or external product credit.

Main objective: Execute the Yang wrapper schedule with fixed durations and explicit assumptions.
Exit criteria: All slots/groups execute; pressures and flows are plausible; all-bed CSS diagnostics are available.
Key artifacts: Yang pilot run package; pressure/composition traces.
Go / no-go risks: No-go if event logic or tank holdup is needed to make the skeleton run.

Main objective: Compute Yang-style metrics from raw external/internal ledgers.
Exit criteria: External feed/product/waste balance closes; internal transfers cancel; all-bed CSS criterion is reported.
Go / no-go risks: No-go if native metrics and Yang-basis metrics are not distinguishable.

Main objective: Protect the simplified architecture and existing toPSAil behavior.
Exit criteria: Two-bed regressions pass; output metadata states no-tank/direct-coupling assumptions.
Go / no-go risks: No-go if old tank/global-scheduler assumptions can reappear unnoticed.

Main objective: Consider events, tank/header holdup, optimization, or generalized PFD only after wrapper path is stable.
Exit criteria: Fixed-duration direct-coupling implementation is documented and tested.
Go / no-go risks: No-go if future refinements are used to mask base-wrapper failures.
